rationale: Round 1 approval no longer fully holds after the doc-sync revision. The core source decomposition still looks good: four beads, all depends_on: [], chain depth 0, B1 explicitly does not delete setup.hasManagedBlock or phase.FindActiveBeadForEpic, and each bead's implementation steps name exact files/symbols/tests. However the round-2 doc-sync fix added shared domain architecture.md paths to multiple work_chunks. That violates this panel lens's explicit zero-shared-key_file_paths requirement and creates real parallel-bead merge risk on workflow architecture.md across all four beads and core architecture.md across B1/B3. The validator still exits with only the known low-redundancy warning, but the stricter decomposition heuristic is not satisfied.
concrete_changes_required: Revise the plan so work_chunks have zero shared key_file_paths. In particular, eliminate the repeated .mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md path across B1/B2/B3/B4 and the repeated .mindspec/domains/core/architecture.md path across B1/B3, while preserving the per-bead doc-sync requirement.

Use bead-unique domain-doc targets under the required .mindspec/domains/<domain>/ trees, or otherwise restructure the doc-sync plan so each bead's declared and intended file surface remains non-overlapping and chain depth stays 0.

findings.detail: Round-2 doc-sync additions make key_file_paths overlap across independent chunks: .mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md appears in Beads 1, 2, 3, and 4; .mindspec/domains/core/architecture.md appears in Beads 1 and 3. This violates the requested zero shared key_file_paths check and increases merge/conflict coordination for beads that otherwise have no dependency edges.

The original R6 source-independence checks still pass: Bead 1 says not to touch setup.hasManagedBlock or phase.FindActiveBeadForEpic, leaving those deletions with Beads 2 and 3; all work_chunks have depends_on: []; bead count remains four; and the implementation steps remain self-contained with exact symbol names and verification commands.
